mapping.a_element: Laplacian of errors governing smooth modes in the codon-to-amino-acid mapping
mapping.b_element: Laplacian on a graph G governing the eigenspace of the second eigenvalue λ2

mapping.a_element: topology of the error-graph (codons connected if likely to be confused)
mapping.b_element: graph G and its genus γ(G)

mapping.a_element: upper bound on the number of possible amino-acids related to the map-coloring problem (chromatic number)
mapping.b_element: Colin de Verdière's upper bound on λ2-multiplicity related to chromatic number chr(γ(G))

mapping.a_element: phase transition in the noisy information channel where the genetic code emerges
mapping.b_element: tight mapping of the graph into an m-dimensional Euclidean space defined by the λ2 eigenspace

brief_justification: Both papers employ the Laplacian spectrum of a graph to derive bounds on dimensionality or multiplicity, with the topology of the graph (error-graph in A, genus in B) constraining the solution via chromatic-number-related limits. The phase transition in A corresponds to the tight mapping in B, both governed by the same underlying spectral machinery.
